agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. Each external system gets an isolated adapter behind a clear boundary, all internal communication flows through domain events on a shared event bus, and Kafka serves double duty as the internal backbone and the Analytics delivery mechanism.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Event Bus (Internal Kafka)","responsibility":"Central nervous system for all domain events. Produces and consumes OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed. Serves as the single source of truth for event ordering.","boundaries":"Internal only. External systems never publish or subscribe directly. All external data enters through ACLs that translate to domain events."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A ACL)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates SAP order confirmations into OrderConfirmed domain events. Handles idempotency via file-tracking (processed file manifest). Manages XML schema evolution and SAP-specific quirks.","boundaries":"Owns the SFTP connection, XML parsing, and SAP-specific data model. Emits only clean domain events. No SAP concepts leak past this boundary."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B ACL)","responsibility":"Two responsibilities: (1) Outbound — listens for ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments. (2) Inbound — exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks, translates them into DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed domain events. Must respond to webhooks within 500ms.","boundaries":"Owns the DHL API contract, authentication, retry logic, and webhook signature verification. No DHL-specific models escape this adapter."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (System C ACL)","responsibility":"Bridges internal Kafka topics to the Analytics Platform's required event stream format. Applies schema transformation, enrichment, and filtering. If Analytics requires a different Kafka cluster or topic structure, this adapter handles the fan-out.","boundaries":"Reads from internal event topics, writes to analytics-specific topics or cluster. Owns the analytics event schema contract."},{"name":"Order Aggregate Service","responsibility":"Core domain logic. Manages order lifecycle (placed → confirmed → shipped → delivered/failed). Enforces business invariants and emits domain events on state transitions.","boundaries":"Owns order state and business rules. Communicates exclusively through domain events and command handlers."},{"name":"Shipment Aggregate Service","responsibility":"Manages shipment lifecycle. Reacts to OrderConfirmed to create shipments, emits ShipmentCreated. Tracks delivery status transitions.","boundaries":"Owns shipment state. Coordinates with DHL Adapter indirectly through events."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Lightweight HTTP ingress layer that receives DHL webhook callbacks, performs immediate signature verification and request validation, enqueues the raw payload onto an internal queue/topic, and returns 200 OK — all within 500ms. Decouples webhook receipt from domain processing.","boundaries":"Owns HTTP concerns only. No business logic. Guarantees fast acknowledgement to DHL."},{"name":"Dead Letter Queue + Retry Processor","responsibility":"Captures failed event processing attempts across all adapters. Provides configurable retry with exponential backoff and alerting on repeated failures.","boundaries":"Cross-cutting infrastructure concern. Each adapter publishes failures here."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces OrderConfirmed events to topic 'order.confirmed'. Schema: {orderId, confirmedAt, lineItems[], sapReference}","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Consumes ShipmentCreated from topic 'shipment.created'. Adapter calls DHL POST /shipments with carrier-specific payload.","coupling_type":"loose — async consumption triggers synchronous outbound API call"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"HTTP POST /webhooks/dhl → raw payload enqueued to topic 'dhl.webhook.raw'. Adapter consumes, translates to DeliveryAttempted|DeliveryCompleted|DeliveryFailed.","coupling_type":"loose — HTTP receipt decoupled from processing via internal queue"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed to respective topics.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Event Bus","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Consumes ALL domain event topics (order.*, shipment.*, delivery.*). Transforms and produces to analytics-specific topics/cluster.","coupling_type":"loose — async, read-only consumer"},{"from":"Order Aggregate Service","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces OrderPlaced on topic 'order.placed'. Consumes OrderConfirmed to advance order state.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Shipment Aggregate Service","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Consumes OrderConfirmed, produces ShipmentCreated. Consumes delivery events to update shipment state.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"}],"data_flow":"1. Order Aggregate emits OrderPlaced → Kafka. 2. SAP Adapter polls SFTP every 15min, retrieves XML, emits OrderConfirmed → Kafka. 3. Order Aggregate consumes OrderConfirmed, advances state. 4. Shipment Aggregate consumes OrderConfirmed, creates shipment, emits ShipmentCreated → Kafka. 5. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated, calls DHL REST API to book. 6. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway returns 200 within 500ms, enqueues raw payload. 7. DHL Adapter consumes raw webhook, translates to DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed → Kafka. 8. Shipment Aggregate consumes delivery events, updates state. 9. Analytics Adapter continuously streams ALL events to Analytics Platform in real-time.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (per external system)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model, protocol, and quirks. ACLs prevent foreign concepts from polluting the core domain. SAP's XML schema, DHL's webhook payload format, and Analytics' event schema are all isolated behind translation boundaries."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Kafka Backbone","rationale":"All three integration requirements converge on events: SAP sends batch confirmations (events), DHL sends status callbacks (events), Analytics needs a real-time event stream. An event backbone unifies these naturally and provides temporal decoupling, replay capability, and a single audit log."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway (Async Handoff)","rationale":"DHL's 500ms SLA for webhook response demands separating HTTP acknowledgement from domain processing. The gateway does minimal work (validate, enqueue, respond) to guarantee fast responses regardless of downstream processing time."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"For the DHL Adapter's outbound calls, use the outbox pattern to ensure that domain events and API calls are eventually consistent. If the DHL API call fails after a ShipmentCreated event is consumed, the outbox ensures retry without event loss."},{"pattern":"Idempotent Consumer","rationale":"All event consumers must be idempotent. SFTP files may be re-polled, DHL webhooks may be retried, Kafka consumers may replay. Every adapter tracks processed message IDs to prevent duplicate side effects."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig (implicit)","rationale":"Each ACL adapter can be independently replaced, versioned, or swapped (e.g., switching from DHL to FedEx) without touching core domain logic. This is the architectural seam for future evolution."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment","DeliveryAttempt"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["SFTPFileManifest","ERPOrderConfirmation"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking","WebhookEvent"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["AnalyticsEventProjection"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"ERP Integration","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — ERP Integration translates SAP XML into OrderConfirmed domain events. Order Management never sees SAP data structures."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Shipment Management dictates the ShipmentCreated contract. Carrier Integration conforms and translates to DHL's API model."},{"upstream":"Carrier Integration","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — DHL webhook payloads are translated into clean delivery domain events before Shipment Management consumes them."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Order events are published in a well-defined domain event schema. Analytics Integration consumes and transforms."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Shipment and delivery events consumed by Analytics in the same published schema."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as both internal event bus and Analytics delivery mechanism","gains":"Single infrastructure component for events. Analytics gets native Kafka integration with minimal latency. Simplified operations.","sacrifices":"Analytics schema requirements may diverge from internal event schemas, requiring a transformation layer. Kafka becomes a single point of failure for the entire system.","alternatives_considered":["Separate message broker (RabbitMQ) for internal events + Kafka only for Analytics — rejected due to operational overhead of two messaging systems","Direct REST API push to Analytics — rejected due to real-time streaming requirement and back-pressure concerns"]},{"decision":"Webhook Gateway as a thin HTTP layer with async handoff","gains":"Guarantees 500ms webhook SLA regardless of domain processing complexity. Clean separation of HTTP concerns from business logic. Can scale the gateway independently.","sacrifices":"Adds a component and an internal topic. Introduces a small window where webhook is acknowledged but not yet processed (at-least-once, not exactly-once from DHL's perspective).","alternatives_considered":["Process webhook synchronously in the DHL Adapter — rejected because domain event emission, database writes, and downstream processing could exceed 500ms","Lambda/serverless function for webhook receipt — viable but adds infrastructure complexity and cold-start risk"]},{"decision":"Polling-based SFTP integration (every 15 minutes) rather than push","gains":"Matches SAP's native integration pattern. Simple, predictable, easy to monitor. No changes required on SAP side.","sacrifices":"15-minute latency floor for order confirmations. Not real-time. Polling window must be carefully managed to avoid duplicate processing.","alternatives_considered":["SAP IDoc push via middleware — rejected as it requires SAP-side configuration changes","File system watchers on SFTP mount — fragile and platform-dependent"]},{"decision":"One ACL per external system (no shared integration layer)","gains":"Each adapter evolves independently. Replacing DHL with FedEx touches only the Carrier Integration context. Fault isolation — SAP SFTP failures don't affect DHL webhooks.","sacrifices":"Some structural duplication across adapters (retry logic, error handling, monitoring). Slightly more code than a unified integration gateway.","alternatives_considered":["Unified ESB/integration platform — rejected due to coupling risk and single point of failure for all integrations","API Gateway with plugins per system — doesn't fit SFTP and event-stream patterns"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Carrier Integration","evolution_path":"The DHL Adapter can be swapped for any carrier (FedEx, UPS) by implementing the same domain event interface. Multi-carrier support is achievable by deploying parallel adapters that each consume ShipmentCreated and route based on carrier selection logic."},{"area":"ERP Integration","evolution_path":"SAP can be replaced with any ERP that can produce order confirmations. The ACL boundary means only the adapter changes. Could also evolve from SFTP polling to real-time API integration without touching core domain."},{"area":"Analytics Integration","evolution_path":"Analytics Platform can be swapped (e.g., Kafka → Snowflake streaming, or BigQuery pub/sub) by replacing only the Analytics Adapter. The internal event schema remains stable."},{"area":"Event Schema","evolution_path":"Domain events should be versioned from day one (e.g., OrderPlaced.v1). Schema registry (Confluent or equivalent) enables backward-compatible evolution without breaking consumers."},{"area":"New External Systems","evolution_path":"Adding a fourth integration (e.g., customer notification service, customs/compliance system) requires only a new adapter that consumes from the event bus. Zero changes to existing components."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain Event Schema","reason":"All adapters and services depend on the domain event contracts. Changing an event schema (e.g., adding required fields to OrderConfirmed) is a coordinated change across producers and consumers. Must be managed via schema registry and versioning."},{"area":"Kafka Topic Structure","reason":"Topic names and partitioning strategy are structural decisions. Changing partition keys affects ordering guarantees. Changing topic names requires coordinated consumer migration."},{"area":"Order and Shipment Aggregate Invariants","reason":"Business rules within aggregates (e.g., an order can only be confirmed once, a shipment cannot transition from Delivered to Created) are intentionally rigid. These protect data integrity and should only change through deliberate domain model evolution."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Kafka cluster failure takes down all integration paths simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Kafka with multi-AZ replication (min 3 brokers, replication factor 3). Implement circuit breakers in adapters with local buffering for short outages. Monitor consumer lag aggressively."},{"risk":"DHL webhook 500ms SLA breach under load","severity":"high","mitigation":"Webhook Gateway is stateless and horizontally scalable. Pre-validate only (signature check, basic schema). Enqueue to Kafka partition and return immediately. Load test to verify p99 < 300ms leaving 200ms headroom."},{"risk":"SFTP polling produces duplicate OrderConfirmed events from reprocessed files","severity":"high","mitigation":"Maintain a processed-file manifest (filename + checksum) in durable storage. Each file is processed exactly once. Additionally, Order Aggregate enforces idempotency — confirming an already-confirmed order is a no-op."},{"risk":"Analytics stream falls behind due to high event volume, creating consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics Adapter uses its own consumer group with independent scaling. Monitor consumer lag with alerts. Kafka retention policy provides replay buffer. Consider dedicated Kafka cluster for analytics if volume diverges significantly."},{"risk":"DHL API outage blocks shipment booking","severity":"high","mitigation":"Transactional outbox pattern ensures ShipmentCreated events are not lost. DHL Adapter retries with exponential backoff. Dead letter queue captures permanently failed bookings for manual intervention. Circuit breaker prevents cascade."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal events and external system contracts","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward/forward). ACL adapters own translation — schema drift in external systems is handled within the adapter without propagating inward."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up Kafka infrastructure, define domain event schemas with a schema registry, implement Order and Shipment Aggregate services with event emission. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel — SAP SFTP Adapter, DHL Carrier Adapter (with Webhook Gateway), Analytics Stream Adapter. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing, load testing the webhook path for 500ms SLA, and chaos testing Kafka failure scenarios. Each adapter should be a separate deployable unit (microservice or at minimum a separate module) to maintain independent deployability.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/events/schemas/ — Domain event Avro/JSON schemas (OrderPlaced.avsc, OrderConfirmed.avsc, ShipmentCreated.avsc, DeliveryAttempted.avsc, DeliveryCompleted.avsc, DeliveryFailed.avsc)","src/adapters/sap/ — SFTP poller, XML parser, file manifest tracker, OrderConfirmed event producer","src/adapters/dhl/ — REST client for shipment booking, webhook gateway HTTP handler, webhook event translator, delivery event producer","src/adapters/analytics/ — Kafka consumer for all domain topics, schema transformer, analytics topic producer","src/domain/order/ — Order aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/domain/shipment/ — Shipment aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/infrastructure/kafka/ — Kafka producer/consumer configuration, topic provisioning, schema registry client","src/infrastructure/outbox/ — Transactional outbox implementation for reliable event production","src/infrastructure/dlq/ — Dead letter queue consumer, retry processor, alerting hooks","docker-compose.yml — Local dev environment with Kafka, Zookeeper, Schema Registry, SFTP server mock"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (ports and adapters) for each service. Domain events as the primary integration mechanism. Each adapter implements a port interface defined by the domain. Outbox pattern for reliable event production. Idempotent consumers everywhere. Circuit breaker on all outbound calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event chain for observability.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT let SAP XML structures or DHL JSON payloads appear in domain events — always translate at the ACL boundary","Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — the 500ms SLA will be violated under load","Do NOT assume SFTP file ordering — files may arrive out of order or be re-uploaded; use idempotency keys","Do NOT share Kafka consumer groups between the Analytics Adapter and internal services — they have different consumption patterns and SLAs","Do NOT skip schema versioning — it feels unnecessary early but becomes critical as soon as any event schema needs to change","Do NOT use a single Kafka topic for all events — use topic-per-event-type for independent scaling and consumer flexibility"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL-per-system pattern is clearly correct given three systems with fundamentally different protocols (SFTP/XML, REST/webhooks, Kafka streaming)","Webhook Gateway async handoff is necessary to meet the 500ms SLA","Kafka as event backbone fits all three integration requirements naturally","Domain event schema as the stable contract between components"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Analytics requires a separate Kafka cluster or can share the internal one — depends on volume and isolation requirements not specified","Exact Kafka partitioning strategy — depends on throughput numbers and ordering requirements not fully specified","Whether SAP sends only confirmations or also other event types that may need handling in future"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns the Kafka infrastructure and can provision topics and consumer groups freely","SAP's SFTP integration is already configured and produces well-formed XML files on a known schedule","DHL's webhook contract includes a signature or HMAC for verification","The Analytics Platform can consume from Kafka topics directly (native Kafka consumer)","Order volume is moderate enough that a single Kafka cluster handles all three integration paths","The 500ms webhook SLA is measured from DHL's perspective (time to receive HTTP 200 response)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. Each external system gets an isolated adapter behind a clear boundary, all internal communication flows through domain events on a shared event bus, and Kafka serves double duty as the internal backbone and the Analytics delivery

content.thinking:
Let me design the integration architecture for this logistics platform with three external systems.

Classification: `none` — sufficient constraints exist. We have clear NFRs (500ms webhook response, 15-min SFTP batch, real-time Kafka stream), well-defined integration boundaries, and specific event types.

These protect data integrity and should only change through deliberate domain model evolution."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Kafka cluster failure takes down all integration paths simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Kafka with multi-AZ replication (min 3 brokers, replication factor 3). Implement circuit breakers in adapters with local buffering for short outages. Monitor consumer lag aggressively."},{"risk":"DHL webhook 500ms SLA breach under load","severity":"high","mitigation":"Webhook Gateway is stateless and horizontally scalable. Pre-validate only (signature check, basic schema). Enqueue to Kafka partition and return immediately. Load test to verify p99 < 300ms leaving 200ms headroom."},{"risk":"SFTP polling produces duplicate OrderConfirmed events from reprocessed files","severity":"high","mitigation":"Maintain a processed-file manifest (filename + checksum) in durable storage. Each file is processed exactly once. Additionally, Order Aggregate enforces idempotency — confirming an already-confirmed order is a no-op."},{"risk":"Analytics stream falls behind due to high event volume, creating consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics Adapter uses its own consumer group with independent scaling. Monitor consumer lag with alerts. Kafka retention policy provides replay buffer. Consider dedicated Kafka cluster for analytics if volume diverges significantly."},{"risk":"DHL API outage blocks shipment booking","severity":"high","mitigation":"Transactional outbox pattern ensures ShipmentCreated events are not lost. DHL Adapter retries with exponential backoff. Dead letter queue captures permanently failed bookings for manual intervention. Circuit breaker prevents cascade."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal events and external system contracts","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward/forward). ACL adapters own translation — schema drift in external systems is handled within the adapter without propagating inward."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up Kafka infrastructure, define domain event schemas with a schema registry, implement Order and Shipment Aggregate services with event emission. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel — SAP SFTP Adapter, DHL Carrier Adapter (with Webhook Gateway), Analytics Stream Adapter. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing, load testing the webhook path for 500ms SLA, and chaos testing Kafka failure scenarios. Each adapter should be a separate deployable unit (microservice or at minimum a separate module) to maintain independent deployability.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/events/schemas/ — Domain event Avro/JSON schemas (OrderPlaced.avsc, OrderConfirmed.avsc, ShipmentCreated.avsc, DeliveryAttempted.avsc, DeliveryCompleted.avsc, DeliveryFailed.avsc)","src/adapters/sap/ — SFTP poller, XML parser, file manifest tracker, OrderConfirmed event producer","src/adapters/dhl/ — REST client for shipment booking, webhook gateway HTTP handler, webhook event translator, delivery event producer","src/adapters/analytics/ — Kafka consumer for all domain topics, schema transformer, analytics topic producer","src/domain/order/ — Order aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/domain/shipment/ — Shipment aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/infrastructure/kafka/ — Kafka producer/consumer configuration, topic provisioning, schema registry client","src/infrastructure/outbox/ — Transactional outbox implementation for reliable event production","src/infrastructure/dlq/ — Dead letter queue consumer, retry processor, alerting hooks","docker-compose.yml — Local dev environment with Kafka, Zookeeper, Schema Registry, SFTP server mock"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (ports and adapters) for each service. Domain events as the primary integration mechanism. Each adapter implements a port interface defined by the domain. Outbox pattern for reliable event production. Idempotent consumers everywhere. Circuit breaker on all outbound calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event chain for observability.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT let SAP XML structures or DHL JSON payloads appear in domain events — always translate at the ACL boundary","Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — the 500ms SLA will be violated under load","Do NOT assume SFTP file ordering — files may arrive out of order or be re-uploaded; use idempotency keys","Do NOT share Kafka consumer groups between the Analytics Adapter and internal services — they have different consumption patterns and SLAs","Do NOT skip schema versioning — it feels unnecessary early but becomes critical as soon as any event schema needs to change","Do NOT use a single Kafka topic for all events — use topic-per-event-type for independent scaling and consumer flexibility"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL-per-system pattern is clearly correct given three systems with fundamentally different protocols (SFTP/XML, REST/webhooks, Kafka streaming)","Webhook Gateway async handoff is necessary to meet the 500ms SLA","Kafka as event backbone fits all three integration requirements naturally","Domain event schema as the stable contract between components"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Analytics requires a separate Kafka cluster or can share the internal one — depends on volume and isolation requirements not specified","Exact Kafka partitioning strategy — depends on throughput numbers and ordering requirements not fully specified","Whether SAP sends only confirmations or also other event types that may need handling in future"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns the Kafka infrastructure and can provision topics and consumer groups freely","SAP's SFTP integration is already configured and produces well-formed XML files on a known schedule","DHL's webhook contract includes a signature or HMAC for verification","The Analytics Platform can consume from Kafka topics directly (native Kafka consumer)","Order volume is moderate enough that a single Kafka cluster handles all three integration paths","The 500ms webhook SLA is measured from DHL's perspective (time to receive HTTP 200 response)"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. Each external system gets an isolated adapter behind a clear boundary, all internal communication flows through domain events on a shared event bus, and Kafka serves double duty as the internal backbone and the Analytics delivery mechanism.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Event Bus (Internal Kafka)","responsibility":"Central nervous system for all domain events. Produces and consumes OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed, ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed. Serves as the single source of truth for event ordering.","boundaries":"Internal only. External systems never publish or subscribe directly. All external data enters through ACLs that translate to domain events."},{"name":"SAP ERP Adapter (System A ACL)","responsibility":"Polls SFTP endpoint every 15 minutes, retrieves XML files, parses and validates them, translates SAP order confirmations into OrderConfirmed domain events. Handles idempotency via file-tracking (processed file manifest). Manages XML schema evolution and SAP-specific quirks.","boundaries":"Owns the SFTP connection, XML parsing, and SAP-specific data model. Emits only clean domain events. No SAP concepts leak past this boundary."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Adapter (System B ACL)","responsibility":"Two responsibilities: (1) Outbound — listens for ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments. (2) Inbound — exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks, translates them into DeliveryAttempted/DeliveryCompleted/DeliveryFailed domain events. Must respond to webhooks within 500ms.","boundaries":"Owns the DHL API contract, authentication, retry logic, and webhook signature verification. No DHL-specific models escape this adapter."},{"name":"Analytics Stream Adapter (System C ACL)","responsibility":"Bridges internal Kafka topics to the Analytics Platform's required event stream format. Applies schema transformation, enrichment, and filtering. If Analytics requires a different Kafka cluster or topic structure, this adapter handles the fan-out.","boundaries":"Reads from internal event topics, writes to analytics-specific topics or cluster. Owns the analytics event schema contract."},{"name":"Order Aggregate Service","responsibility":"Core domain logic. Manages order lifecycle (placed → confirmed → shipped → delivered/failed). Enforces business invariants and emits domain events on state transitions.","boundaries":"Owns order state and business rules. Communicates exclusively through domain events and command handlers."},{"name":"Shipment Aggregate Service","responsibility":"Manages shipment lifecycle. Reacts to OrderConfirmed to create shipments, emits ShipmentCreated. Tracks delivery status transitions.","boundaries":"Owns shipment state. Coordinates with DHL Adapter indirectly through events."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Lightweight HTTP ingress layer that receives DHL webhook callbacks, performs immediate signature verification and request validation, enqueues the raw payload onto an internal queue/topic, and returns 200 OK — all within 500ms. Decouples webhook receipt from domain processing.","boundaries":"Owns HTTP concerns only. No business logic. Guarantees fast acknowledgement to DHL."},{"name":"Dead Letter Queue + Retry Processor","responsibility":"Captures failed event processing attempts across all adapters. Provides configurable retry with exponential backoff and alerting on repeated failures.","boundaries":"Cross-cutting infrastructure concern. Each adapter publishes failures here."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"SAP ERP Adapter","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces OrderConfirmed events to topic 'order.confirmed'. Schema: {orderId, confirmedAt, lineItems[], sapReference}","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"Consumes ShipmentCreated from topic 'shipment.created'. Adapter calls DHL POST /shipments with carrier-specific payload.","coupling_type":"loose — async consumption triggers synchronous outbound API call"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Adapter","contract":"HTTP POST /webhooks/dhl → raw payload enqueued to topic 'dhl.webhook.raw'. Adapter consumes, translates to DeliveryAttempted|DeliveryCompleted|DeliveryFailed.","coupling_type":"loose — HTTP receipt decoupled from processing via internal queue"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Adapter","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed to respective topics.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Event Bus","to":"Analytics Stream Adapter","contract":"Consumes ALL domain event topics (order.*, shipment.*, delivery.*). Transforms and produces to analytics-specific topics/cluster.","coupling_type":"loose — async, read-only consumer"},{"from":"Order Aggregate Service","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Produces OrderPlaced on topic 'order.placed'. Consumes OrderConfirmed to advance order state.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"},{"from":"Shipment Aggregate Service","to":"Event Bus","contract":"Consumes OrderConfirmed, produces ShipmentCreated. Consumes delivery events to update shipment state.","coupling_type":"loose — async, event-driven"}],"data_flow":"1. Order Aggregate emits OrderPlaced → Kafka. 2. SAP Adapter polls SFTP every 15min, retrieves XML, emits OrderConfirmed → Kafka. 3. Order Aggregate consumes OrderConfirmed, advances state. 4. Shipment Aggregate consumes OrderConfirmed, creates shipment, emits ShipmentCreated → Kafka. 5. DHL Adapter consumes ShipmentCreated, calls DHL REST API to book. 6. DHL sends webhook callbacks → Webhook Gateway returns 200 within 500ms, enqueues raw payload. 7. DHL Adapter consumes raw webhook, translates to DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed → Kafka. 8. Shipment Aggregate consumes delivery events, updates state. 9. Analytics Adapter continuously streams ALL events to Analytics Platform in real-time.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (per external system)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model, protocol, and quirks. ACLs prevent foreign concepts from polluting the core domain. SAP's XML schema, DHL's webhook payload format, and Analytics' event schema are all isolated behind translation boundaries."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Kafka Backbone","rationale":"All three integration requirements converge on events: SAP sends batch confirmations (events), DHL sends status callbacks (events), Analytics needs a real-time event stream. An event backbone unifies these naturally and provides temporal decoupling, replay capability, and a single audit log."},{"pattern":"Webhook Gateway (Async Handoff)","rationale":"DHL's 500ms SLA for webhook response demands separating HTTP acknowledgement from domain processing. The gateway does minimal work (validate, enqueue, respond) to guarantee fast responses regardless of downstream processing time."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"For the DHL Adapter's outbound calls, use the outbox pattern to ensure that domain events and API calls are eventually consistent. If the DHL API call fails after a ShipmentCreated event is consumed, the outbox ensures retry without event loss."},{"pattern":"Idempotent Consumer","rationale":"All event consumers must be idempotent. SFTP files may be re-polled, DHL webhooks may be retried, Kafka consumers may replay. Every adapter tracks processed message IDs to prevent duplicate side effects."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig (implicit)","rationale":"Each ACL adapter can be independently replaced, versioned, or swapped (e.g., switching from DHL to FedEx) without touching core domain logic. This is the architectural seam for future evolution."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment","DeliveryAttempt"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["SFTPFileManifest","ERPOrderConfirmation"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking","WebhookEvent"]},{"name":"Analytics Integration","classification":"generic","aggregates":["AnalyticsEventProjection"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"ERP Integration","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — ERP Integration translates SAP XML into OrderConfirmed domain events. Order Management never sees SAP data structures."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Shipment Management dictates the ShipmentCreated contract. Carrier Integration conforms and translates to DHL's API model."},{"upstream":"Carrier Integration","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — DHL webhook payloads are translated into clean delivery domain events before Shipment Management consumes them."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Order events are published in a well-defined domain event schema. Analytics Integration consumes and transforms."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Integration","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Shipment and delivery events consumed by Analytics in the same published schema."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Kafka as both internal event bus and Analytics delivery mechanism","gains":"Single infrastructure component for events. Analytics gets native Kafka integration with minimal latency. Simplified operations.","sacrifices":"Analytics schema requirements may diverge from internal event schemas, requiring a transformation layer. Kafka becomes a single point of failure for the entire system.","alternatives_considered":["Separate message broker (RabbitMQ) for internal events + Kafka only for Analytics — rejected due to operational overhead of two messaging systems","Direct REST API push to Analytics — rejected due to real-time streaming requirement and back-pressure concerns"]},{"decision":"Webhook Gateway as a thin HTTP layer with async handoff","gains":"Guarantees 500ms webhook SLA regardless of domain processing complexity. Clean separation of HTTP concerns from business logic. Can scale the gateway independently.","sacrifices":"Adds a component and an internal topic. Introduces a small window where webhook is acknowledged but not yet processed (at-least-once, not exactly-once from DHL's perspective).","alternatives_considered":["Process webhook synchronously in the DHL Adapter — rejected because domain event emission, database writes, and downstream processing could exceed 500ms","Lambda/serverless function for webhook receipt — viable but adds infrastructure complexity and cold-start risk"]},{"decision":"Polling-based SFTP integration (every 15 minutes) rather than push","gains":"Matches SAP's native integration pattern. Simple, predictable, easy to monitor. No changes required on SAP side.","sacrifices":"15-minute latency floor for order confirmations. Not real-time. Polling window must be carefully managed to avoid duplicate processing.","alternatives_considered":["SAP IDoc push via middleware — rejected as it requires SAP-side configuration changes","File system watchers on SFTP mount — fragile and platform-dependent"]},{"decision":"One ACL per external system (no shared integration layer)","gains":"Each adapter evolves independently. Replacing DHL with FedEx touches only the Carrier Integration context. Fault isolation — SAP SFTP failures don't affect DHL webhooks.","sacrifices":"Some structural duplication across adapters (retry logic, error handling, monitoring). Slightly more code than a unified integration gateway.","alternatives_considered":["Unified ESB/integration platform — rejected due to coupling risk and single point of failure for all integrations","API Gateway with plugins per system — doesn't fit SFTP and event-stream patterns"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Carrier Integration","evolution_path":"The DHL Adapter can be swapped for any carrier (FedEx, UPS) by implementing the same domain event interface. Multi-carrier support is achievable by deploying parallel adapters that each consume ShipmentCreated and route based on carrier selection logic."},{"area":"ERP Integration","evolution_path":"SAP can be replaced with any ERP that can produce order confirmations. The ACL boundary means only the adapter changes. Could also evolve from SFTP polling to real-time API integration without touching core domain."},{"area":"Analytics Integration","evolution_path":"Analytics Platform can be swapped (e.g., Kafka → Snowflake streaming, or BigQuery pub/sub) by replacing only the Analytics Adapter. The internal event schema remains stable."},{"area":"Event Schema","evolution_path":"Domain events should be versioned from day one (e.g., OrderPlaced.v1). Schema registry (Confluent or equivalent) enables backward-compatible evolution without breaking consumers."},{"area":"New External Systems","evolution_path":"Adding a fourth integration (e.g., customer notification service, customs/compliance system) requires only a new adapter that consumes from the event bus. Zero changes to existing components."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain Event Schema","reason":"All adapters and services depend on the domain event contracts. Changing an event schema (e.g., adding required fields to OrderConfirmed) is a coordinated change across producers and consumers. Must be managed via schema registry and versioning."},{"area":"Kafka Topic Structure","reason":"Topic names and partitioning strategy are structural decisions. Changing partition keys affects ordering guarantees. Changing topic names requires coordinated consumer migration."},{"area":"Order and Shipment Aggregate Invariants","reason":"Business rules within aggregates (e.g., an order can only be confirmed once, a shipment cannot transition from Delivered to Created) are intentionally rigid. These protect data integrity and should only change through deliberate domain model evolution."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Kafka cluster failure takes down all integration paths simultaneously","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Deploy Kafka with multi-AZ replication (min 3 brokers, replication factor 3). Implement circuit breakers in adapters with local buffering for short outages. Monitor consumer lag aggressively."},{"risk":"DHL webhook 500ms SLA breach under load","severity":"high","mitigation":"Webhook Gateway is stateless and horizontally scalable. Pre-validate only (signature check, basic schema). Enqueue to Kafka partition and return immediately. Load test to verify p99 < 300ms leaving 200ms headroom."},{"risk":"SFTP polling produces duplicate OrderConfirmed events from reprocessed files","severity":"high","mitigation":"Maintain a processed-file manifest (filename + checksum) in durable storage. Each file is processed exactly once. Additionally, Order Aggregate enforces idempotency — confirming an already-confirmed order is a no-op."},{"risk":"Analytics stream falls behind due to high event volume, creating consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics Adapter uses its own consumer group with independent scaling. Monitor consumer lag with alerts. Kafka retention policy provides replay buffer. Consider dedicated Kafka cluster for analytics if volume diverges significantly."},{"risk":"DHL API outage blocks shipment booking","severity":"high","mitigation":"Transactional outbox pattern ensures ShipmentCreated events are not lost. DHL Adapter retries with exponential backoff. Dead letter queue captures permanently failed bookings for manual intervention. Circuit breaker prevents cascade."},{"risk":"Schema drift between internal events and external system contracts","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Schema registry with compatibility checks (backward/forward). ACL adapters own translation — schema drift in external systems is handled within the adapter without propagating inward."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up Kafka infrastructure, define domain event schemas with a schema registry, implement Order and Shipment Aggregate services with event emission. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel — SAP SFTP Adapter, DHL Carrier Adapter (with Webhook Gateway), Analytics Stream Adapter. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing, load testing the webhook path for 500ms SLA, and chaos testing Kafka failure scenarios. Each adapter should be a separate deployable unit (microservice or at minimum a separate module) to maintain independent deployability.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/events/schemas/ — Domain event Avro/JSON schemas (OrderPlaced.avsc, OrderConfirmed.avsc, ShipmentCreated.avsc, DeliveryAttempted.avsc, DeliveryCompleted.avsc, DeliveryFailed.avsc)","src/adapters/sap/ — SFTP poller, XML parser, file manifest tracker, OrderConfirmed event producer","src/adapters/dhl/ — REST client for shipment booking, webhook gateway HTTP handler, webhook event translator, delivery event producer","src/adapters/analytics/ — Kafka consumer for all domain topics, schema transformer, analytics topic producer","src/domain/order/ — Order aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/domain/shipment/ — Shipment aggregate, command handlers, event handlers","src/infrastructure/kafka/ — Kafka producer/consumer configuration, topic provisioning, schema registry client","src/infrastructure/outbox/ — Transactional outbox implementation for reliable event production","src/infrastructure/dlq/ — Dead letter queue consumer, retry processor, alerting hooks","docker-compose.yml — Local dev environment with Kafka, Zookeeper, Schema Registry, SFTP server mock"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture (ports and adapters) for each service. Domain events as the primary integration mechanism. Each adapter implements a port interface defined by the domain. Outbox pattern for reliable event production. Idempotent consumers everywhere. Circuit breaker on all outbound calls. Structured logging with correlation IDs across the entire event chain for observability.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT let SAP XML structures or DHL JSON payloads appear in domain events — always translate at the ACL boundary","Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously in the HTTP handler — the 500ms SLA will be violated under load","Do NOT assume SFTP file ordering — files may arrive out of order or be re-uploaded; use idempotency keys","Do NOT share Kafka consumer groups between the Analytics Adapter and internal services — they have different consumption patterns and SLAs","Do NOT skip schema versioning — it feels unnecessary early but becomes critical as soon as any event schema needs to change","Do NOT use a single Kafka topic for all events — use topic-per-event-type for independent scaling and consumer flexibility"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["ACL-per-system pattern is clearly correct given three systems with fundamentally different protocols (SFTP/XML, REST/webhooks, Kafka streaming)","Webhook Gateway async handoff is necessary to meet the 500ms SLA","Kafka as event backbone fits all three integration requirements naturally","Domain event schema as the stable contract between components"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Analytics requires a separate Kafka cluster or can share the internal one — depends on volume and isolation requirements not specified","Exact Kafka partitioning strategy — depends on throughput numbers and ordering requirements not fully specified","Whether SAP sends only confirmations or also other event types that may need handling in future"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns the Kafka infrastructure and can provision topics and consumer groups freely","SAP's SFTP integration is already configured and produces well-formed XML files on a known schedule","DHL's webhook contract includes a signature or HMAC for verification","The Analytics Platform can consume from Kafka topics directly (native Kafka consumer)","Order volume is moderate enough that a single Kafka cluster handles all three integration paths","The 500ms webhook SLA is measured from DHL's perspective (time to receive HTTP 200 response)"]}}
